description_long: This water point consists of two large, circular basins made of weathered metal, likely bronze or copper, with a patina that gives them a greenish-blue hue. The basins are mounted on a cylindrical pedestal and are connected, with one slightly higher than the other, allowing water to flow from the upper to the lower basin. A single, curved metal spout rises from the base and extends over the upper basin, serving as the water outlet. The basins are set on a cobblestone platform, which includes a drainage grate nearby for water runoff. The structure is robust and utilitarian, designed for public use in an urban environment. The overall appearance is functional yet visually striking due to the aged metal and the interplay of circular forms.
description: A dual-basin metal water point with a curved spout, mounted on a cylindrical pedestal and set on a cobblestone platform with a drainage grate. The basins have a weathered greenish-blue patina, giving the structure a distinctive urban character.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the two basins from an angle, highlighting the depth of the upper basin and the curved spout extending over it, as well as the cobblestone base and drainage grate.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a frontal perspective, clearly displaying the spout's orientation over the upper basin and the cylindrical pedestal supporting both basins.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a side view, emphasizing the staggered heights of the basins and the connection between them, as well as the patina on the metal surfaces.
description_view_3: View 3 shows the rear of the water point, focusing on the back of the basins and the continuous cylindrical support, with the spout visible from behind.
attribute_spout_type: curved gooseneck pipe, vertically mounted and extending horizontally over the upper basin
